title: cd — Climate Departure
text: Two products: - reanalysis-era5-land-monthly-means — tmean, prcp, dewpoint, soil moisture (returns zip→GRIB) - derived-era5-land-daily-statistics — tmax, tmin (returns NetCDF, needs day + time_zone params) Key gotchas: - Temperature Kelvin (subtract 273.15) - Precipitation m/day (× 1000 × days_in_month → mm/month) - GRIB metadata lies units (says °C, values K) - retry = 120 avoids rate limiting (default 5 aggressive) - wf_request_batch() parallel requests (20 workers)

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: bundled AOI FWCP Peace Region polygon (single multi-polygon, EPSG:4326). sf polygon works. watersheds within region drain Mackenzie River — Peace flows east Slave, flows Mackenzie, empties Beaufort Sea Arctic Ocean. Arctic-draining side British Columbia, distinct Pacific-draining watersheds south west Continental Divide. also carry BC ecoregions analysis. Ecoregions partition province broad climate-physiography zones based latitude, elevation, dominant vegetation. Climate departure can vary across ways regional average hides, use later sub-region per-ecoregion breakdown. FWCP Peace Region area interest (~73,000 km^2) northeastern British Columbia, coloured BC ecoregion. Williston Reservoir dominates basin.
code: library(cd) library(sf)  aoi <- st_read(   system.file("extdata", "example_aoi_fwcp_peace.gpkg", package = "cd"),   quiet = TRUE )  area_km2 <- as.numeric(sum(st_area(st_transform(aoi, 3005)))) / 1e6 aoi_bb <- st_bbox(aoi)  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = ecoregions, aes(fill = name_tc), color = "grey45",           linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.45) +   geom_sf(data = aoi, fill = NA, color = "#2166ac", linewidth = 0.8) +   geom_sf(data = lakes, fill = "#a6bddb", color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = rivers, fill = "#a6bddb", color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = streams, color = "#74a9cf", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +   geom_sf(data = highways, color = "#333333", linewidth = 0.5) +   geom_sf(data = towns, color = "black", size = 2.5) +   ggrepel::geom_label_repel(     data = towns,     aes(label = name, geometry = geom),     stat = "sf_coordinates",     size = 3.2, fill = "white", alpha = 0.85,     label.padding = unit(0.2, "lines"),     min.segment.length = 0   ) +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3", name = "Ecoregion") +   guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE)) +   coord_sf(     xlim = c(aoi_bb["xmin"] - 0.4, aoi_bb["xmax"] + 0.4),     ylim = c(aoi_bb["ymin"] - 0.5, aoi_bb["ymax"] + 0.3)   ) +   labs(title = "FWCP Peace Region",        subtitle = paste0(round(area_km2), " km^2 — ", nrow(ecoregions), " ecoregions")) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +   theme(axis.title = element_blank(),         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.text = element_text(size = 8),         legend.title = element_text(size = 9),         legend.key.size = unit(0.4, "cm"))

Alongside GeoTIFFs publish static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) — small set JSON files index assets. GeoTIFFs catalog JSON live https://stac-era5-land.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catalog.json. cd_catalog() reads URL default. Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs also usable directly outside R — example, QGIS via STAC plugin, gdalcubes, STAC-aware client.

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: Anomalies computed pre-warming reference period — 1951–1980, three decades climate change accelerated. Saying year “+1.5 °C” means 1.5 °C warmer average year 1951 1980. trend table follows two rows per variable. compute trends two different start years: 1951–present (75 years) — long view. Captures full magnitude warming since pre-warming reference. 1981–present (45 years) — starts beginning World Meteorological Organization’s recent 30-year “climate normal” (1981–2010). reference period used published climate products, makes results easy compare Intergovernmental Panel Climate Change reports government climate summaries. Comparing two slopes informative. 45-year slope steeper 75-year slope, warming accelerated — recent decades heating faster long-term average. 45-year slope shallower, warming slowed (though “slower” almost never means “stopped”). two slopes similar, rate change roughly steady across full record. Total Change slope multiplied number years — cumulative shift trend window.  Annual mean temperature anomaly FWCP Peace Region relative 1951-1980 baseline. Annual precipitation anomaly (% 1951-1980 baseline) FWCP Peace Region.

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: cd package ships daytime maximum (tmax) overnight minimum (tmin) temperatures alongside daily mean. carry distinct information. Overnight minimums warming faster daytime maximums — “day-night asymmetry” — one textbook fingerprints greenhouse warming (Karl et al. 1993). Whether watershed region shows signal depends local geography (valley inversions, snow cover, slope-aspect mix). Annual daytime maximum temperature (tmax) anomaly FWCP Peace Region relative 1951-1980 baseline. Annual overnight minimum temperature (tmin) anomaly FWCP Peace Region relative 1951-1980 baseline. Diurnal temperature range (daytime maximum minus overnight minimum) annual mean FWCP Peace Region. downward trend indicates overnight lows warming faster daytime highs — textbook day-night asymmetry shows . FWCP Peace Region, overnight minimums warming faster daytime maximums — textbook day-night asymmetry. Daytime maximums warmed +0.027 °C per year since 1951 (+2.0 °C cumulative), overnight minimums warmed +0.032 °C per year (+2.4 °C cumulative). overnight side warmed roughly 0.4 °C daytime side full record, narrowing diurnal temperature range amount.
code: trn_tmax <- cd_trend(   ano[ano$variable == "tmax" & ano$period == "annual", ],   trend_start = c(1951, 1981) ) cd_plot_timeseries(ano, variable = "tmax", period = "annual", trend = trn_tmax,   title = "Daytime Maximum (tmax) — Annual Anomaly") trn_tmin <- cd_trend(   ano[ano$variable == "tmin" & ano$period == "annual", ],   trend_start = c(1951, 1981) ) cd_plot_timeseries(ano, variable = "tmin", period = "annual", trend = trn_tmin,   title = "Overnight Minimum (tmin) — Annual Anomaly") tmax_ts <- ts[ts$variable == "tmax" & ts$period == "annual", c("year", "value")] tmin_ts <- ts[ts$variable == "tmin" & ts$period == "annual", c("year", "value")] dtr <- merge(tmax_ts, tmin_ts, by = "year", suffixes = c("_max", "_min")) dtr$dtr <- dtr$value_max - dtr$value_min  ggplot(dtr, aes(x = year, y = dtr)) +   geom_line(color = "grey50") +   geom_point(color = "grey30", size = 1) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "#b2182b", linewidth = 0.8) +   labs(     title = "Diurnal Temperature Range — FWCP Peace Region",     x = NULL,     y = expression("Daytime maximum minus overnight minimum (" * degree * "C)")   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: tables compare two windows directly — recent decade (2015–2025) pre-warming reference (1951–1980). row includes long-term Mann-Kendall p-value trend test (75-year window) can see whether variable also statistically significant monotonic trend. small p-value (rule thumb: < 0.05) means trend unlikely noise. trend test asks slightly different question “two windows different” — tests steady directional change — closest available proxy now.
code: # Equivalent to: #   cmp <- cd_compare(ts, window_a = 2015:2025, window_b = 1951:1980, #                     method = "mean_diff") cmp <- merge(cmp, trn_p, by = c("variable", "period"), all.x = TRUE) kableExtra::kable_styling(   knitr::kable(cmp, caption = NA, digits = 2),   bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed") ) |>   kableExtra::scroll_box(height = "320px") # Equivalent to: #   cmp_pct <- cd_compare( #     ts[ts$variable %in% c("prcp", "soil_moisture"), ], #     window_a = 2015:2025, window_b = 1951:1980, method = "pct_change") cmp_pct <- merge(cmp_pct, trn_p, by = c("variable", "period"), all.x = TRUE) knitr::kable(cmp_pct, caption = NA, digits = 1)

title: Climate Departure for the FWCP Peace Region
text: Three findings emerge FWCP Peace Region trends. Warming broad, fast, uniform. five ecoregions warmed +1.7 °C +1.9 °C cumulative since 1951, rate near +0.3 °C per decade, Mann-Kendall p-values 0.001 every ecoregion. trend statistically significant beyond reasonable doubt random noise. Daily maximum, daily minimum, daily mean temperatures tell story. thermal hot spot. whole region moved together. subtle gradient emerge — northern, higher-elevation ecoregions (Omineca Mountains, Boreal Mountains Plateaus) warmed 0.2 °C southern Fraser Basin, consistent well-documented pattern high-latitude high-elevation amplification — small relative regional signal. Precipitation increasing significantly two northernmost ecoregions. Across FWCP Peace whole, long-term trend annual precipitation statistically significant. Broken ecoregion, Boreal Mountains Plateaus Northern Canadian Rocky Mountains — northern half region — show real increases annual precipitation (p = 0.02 0.01). southern central ecoregions show significant change. north-versus-south contrast regional average hides, place breaking analysis ecoregion worth . atmosphere drying despite, places, precipitation. Vapour pressure deficit — gap much water air hold much actually — rose significantly every ecoregion (p < 0.005 across five). Warmer air holds water saturating, pulls moisture soil vegetation evaporation transpiration. Soil moisture essentially flat every ecoregion — even precipitation increased — warmer atmosphere drinking extra water back. headline ecological finding region: water inputs may rising places, soil vegetation seeing available moisture. cold-water salmonids FWCP supports — bull trout, Arctic grayling, mountain whitefish, rainbow trout — signals point direction. title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: cd fetches ERA5-Land hourly reanalysis (1950–present, ~9 km native grid) DestinE Earth Data Hub, subsets British Columbia, aggregates monthly, seasonal, annual periods, derives additional variables (vapour pressure deficit relative humidity temperature + dewpoint via Tetens equation, soil moisture 4-depth mean), writes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs alongside static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) public S3 bucket. monthly GitHub Action keeps catalog current. consumer side, R functions (cd_catalog, cd_extract, cd_baseline, cd_anomaly, cd_trend, cd_compare, cd_summary) read COGs directly via GDAL’s /vsicurl/ — credentials, tile server — crop user-supplied area interest, compute baselines anomalies arbitrary reference periods, run Mann-Kendall Theil-Sen trend statistics. baseline comparison logic stays consumer side, reference periods baked served data.

title: Climate Departure Analysis from ERA5-Land Reanalysis
text: producer pipeline fetches ERA5-Land hourly reanalysis DestinE Earth Data Hub, derives additional variables (VPD, RH, soil moisture), aggregates monthly, seasonal, annual periods single EPSG:4326 BC grid, writes Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs alongside static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) public S3 bucket. Catalog (JSON): https://stac-era5-land.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/catalog.json Region: BC (~48–60° N, 114–140° W), 1950–2025, ~9 km native grid Variables: tmean, tmax, tmin, prcp, vpd, rh, soil_moisture Periods: monthly, seasonal (DJF/MAM/JJA/SON), annual catalog consumable directly outside R — example, QGIS via STAC plugin, gdalcubes, STAC-aware client. cd_catalog() consumer function reads catalog URL default.

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: Downloads ERA5-Land data Copernicus Climate Data Store (CDS) specified bounding box, time period, set variables. Uses two CDS products: monthly means variables, daily statistics tmax/tmin (available monthly means).

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
code: cd_fetch(   years,   months = 1:12,   variables = cd_variables()$variable,   bbox = c(60, -140, 48, -114),   output_dir,   source = "era5_land",   force = FALSE,   retry = 60 )

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: years Integer vector years download. months Integer vector months (1-12). Default 1:12. variables Character vector cd variable names fetch raw inputs . Default fetches inputs needed 7 variables. bbox Numeric vector c(north, west, south, east) degrees. Default covers British Columbia. output_dir Character path write downloaded files. source Character. Data source identifier. Currently "era5_land" supported. force Logical. Re-download even files exist. Default FALSE. retry Numeric. Polling interval seconds waiting CDS jobs. Default 60. Increase hitting rate limits (429 errors).

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch
text: Character vector downloaded file paths (GRIBs extracted zip archives).

title: Download ERA5-Land climate data — cd_fetch

title: cd 0.1.4 (2026-05-01)
text: KOTL vignette removed. peace-fwcp now canonical worked example — covers everything KOTL higher fidelity (regional + per-ecoregion + day-night asymmetry + plain-language explainers + interpretation), dropping second live-S3 vignette saves ~60 s per pkgdown render removes last /vsicurl/ flake surface doc build. KOTL polygon assets stay inst/extdata/ README quick-start still uses . Future direction (single-vignette snowpack story split vignettes AOI scale) deferred snow-pack variables land — see #49. (#50)

title: cd 0.1.3 (2026-05-01)
text: CI fragility patch. peace-fwcp vignette previously re-fetched ~144 /vsicurl/ COG range requests every pkgdown render; one transient flake failed whole build. Heavy data now pre-computed data-raw/peace_fwcp_vignette_data.R shipped inst/vignette-data/ (160 KB rds + 6 KB tif). Vignette loads via system.file() renders ~10 s instead ~6 min. Live cd_catalog() read kept consumer entry-point demonstration. (#45)

title: cd 0.1.2 (2026-04-30)
text: Vignette docs patch. New peace-fwcp vignette runs consumer pipeline regional administrative AOI (FWCP Peace Region, ~73,000 km², ~11x KOTL) — catalog → extract → trends → recent vs pre-warming → spatial map → per-ecoregion breakdown across five BC ecoregions intersecting region, faceted time-series carrying 75-yr 45-yr Theil-Sen trend lines, wide roll-table, day-night asymmetry section (textbook signal show , unlike KOTL), three-finding interpretation. Plain-language explainers trend windows, WMO climate normal, “warming accelerated/slowed” framing. README gains Data section catalog URL /vsicurl/ direct-read pattern COGs usable outside R (QGIS, gdalcubes, rasterio). Issue #43 filed cd_compare() gain proper window-vs-window p-value. (#42)

title: cd 0.1.1 (2026-04-15)
text: Vignette docs patch. climate-departure vignette gained “Daytime Highs Overnight Lows” section using tmax/tmin variables now STAC, honest framing example watershed (textbook day-night asymmetry doesn’t show Kootenay Lake — dominant signal summer daytime maximum, temperature envelope salmonid thermal stress tributaries). Existing maps now clip context layers mask departure rasters watershed group polygon tighter framing. Interpretation section corrected: precipitation declined ~10% (statistically significant) soils drying due falling precipitation rising evapotranspiration. README quick-start fixed (referencing files don’t exist) now links live pkgdown vignette. (#39)

title: cd 0.1.0 (2026-04-14)
text: CRAN release: 2020-10-22 First minor release. Producer pipeline migrated Copernicus CDS DestinE Earth Data Hub (Zarr). ERA5-Land data 9 km native grid, rate limiting, ~5x faster fetches. 7 cd variables (tmax, tmin, tmean, prcp, vpd, rh, soil_moisture) regenerated single internally-consistent EPSG:4326 BC grid. Monthly GitHub Action rewired use EDH. Consumer API unchanged — cd_catalog() friends work exactly refreshed STAC catalog s3://stac-era5-land. See pkgdown reference current function list. (#36)

title: cd 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial development version. Consumer producer pipelines ERA5-Land climate departure analysis.
